title: pavonia hookeri
intro: Pavonia hookeri, also known as Hooker's rosemallow, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is a member of the Malvaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The leaves and roots of Pavonia hookeri have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
